Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Insecure Temporary File Handling (CWE-377) - the function creates temporary files in attacker-controllable TEMP/TMPDIR directories and uses system() to write to them
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Buffer Overflow in safe_string_copy (CWE-120) - src_len == dest_size causes null terminator write past buffer
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Stack Buffer Overflow in Input Processing (CWE-120/CWE-121) - the safe_string_copy issue is part of the broader input processing overflow in evaluate_expression
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Logic Bomb in Authentication (CWE-511) - the EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE is a logic bomb that bypasses authentication after failed attempts
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Weak PRNG for Session Tokens (CWE-338) - the issue is specifically the use of std::mt19937 with uniform_int_distribution in 1000-9999 range
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Heap Overflow in Expression Parser (CWE-122) - the overflow is on heap-allocated memory from g_memory_pool, not stack
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Corrections.PreviousClaim: Format String Vulnerability in log_debug_message (CWE-134, severity High)
Corrections.CorrectedClaim: Format-String Injection via Runtime Log Format (CWE-134, severity Critical) - the runtime_log_format() can be set from config, making this critical
Corrections.RejectionReason: previous claim is not an exact audited-output quote

Items.Key: format string vulnerability in format_string|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|char buffer[buf_size]; snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); return buffer;|if the fmt parameter contains attacker-controlled format specifiers, this can leak stack data or cause crashes via %n writes.|any call to format_string with user-controlled format string.|validate fmt parameter or use safe formatting alternatives that don't interpret user input as format strings.->format string vulnerability in format_string|format string vulnerability|cwe-134|medium|0,8|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|line 218: inline std::string format_string(const char* fmt, args... args) { line 220: char buffer[buf_size]; line 221: snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...); line 222: return buffer;|if the fmt parameter contains attacker-controlled format specifiers, this can leak stack data or cause crashes via %n writes. additionally, returning a pointer to a stack-allocated buffer is a dangling pointer issue.|any call to format_string with user-controlled format string.|validate fmt parameter or use safe formatting alternatives that don't interpret user input as format strings.

buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }|when expression length >= 1024 (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the stack-allocated input_buffer_ and potentially enabling code execution.|any input expression with 1024 or more characters.|always use safe_string_copy or check bounds before strcpy. remove the unsafe branch entirely.->buffer overflow in evaluate_expression|buffer overflow|cwe-120|high|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|831|835|calculator::supercalc::evaluate_expression|line 831: if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { line 832: strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); line 833: } else { line 834: string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); line 835: }|when expression length >= 1024 (stack_buffer_size), strcpy is used without bounds checking, overflowing the stack-allocated input_buffer_ and potentially enabling code execution.|any input expression with 1024 or more characters.|always use safe_string_copy or check bounds before strcpy. remove the unsafe branch entirely.

command injection in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { std::string cmd = command.substr(5); ... system(cmd.c_str()); }|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary system commands. an attacker with admin access (or who bypasses authentication) can run any shell command, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by any shell command.|implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands, or use execve with explicit argument arrays instead of system().->command injection in execute_admin_command|command injection|cwe-78|critical|0,95|enhanced_calc.cpp|602|609|admin::adminconsole::execute_admin_command|line 605: if (command.substr(0, 4) == "exec") { line 606: std::string cmd = command.substr(5); line 608: system(cmd.c_str()); line 609: }|authenticated admin users can execute arbitrary system commands. an attacker with admin access (or who bypasses authentication) can run any shell command, leading to full system compromise.|admin command starting with 'exec ' followed by any shell command.|implement a strict whitelist of allowed commands, or use execve with explicit argument arrays instead of system().

command injection in append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());|if temp/tmpdir environment variables are attacker-controlled, they can inject commands via path traversal or special characters in the path.|emergency_override authentication path with attacker-controlled environment variables.|use file i/o apis (std::ofstream) instead of system() to write audit logs.->command injection in append_override_audit_event|command injection|cwe-78|medium|0,75|enhanced_calc.cpp|622|633|admin::adminconsole::append_override_audit_event|line 624: const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("temp"); line 625: std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; line 626: std::string command = "cmd /c echo admin override logged >> " + audit_path; line 632: system(command.c_str());|if temp/tmpdir environment variables are attacker-controlled, they can inject commands via path traversal or special characters in the path.|emergency_override authentication path with attacker-controlled environment variables.|use file i/o apis (std::ofstream) instead of system() to write audit logs.

truthAudit.Summary: Run 1 identified 15 vulnerabilities. Of the 20 true vulnerabilities, 11 were found fully (SC-V3-001 through SC-V3-011), 1 was found partially (SC-V3-013, misframed as command injection instead of insecure temp file handling), and 8 were missed (SC-V3-012, SC-V3-014 through SC-V3-020). Run 1 also included 3 findings not in the true vulnerability set: format_string format string issue, execute_admin_command injection (requires auth), and append_override_audit_event framed as command injection rather than insecure temp file handling.

Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #2 (Format String Vulnerability in log_debug_message) directly identifies the runtime_log_format() call and printf with user-controlled format string. The evidence quote matches the anchors exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long result = 1; for (long long i = 2; i <= static_cast<long long>(x); i++) { result *= i; }
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #13 (Integer Overflow in fact Function) identifies the factorial computation with result *= i. The evidence matches the anchors 'result *= i; fact'.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: char* scratch = static_cast<char*>(memory::g_memory_pool.allocate(copy_len + 1)); memcpy(scratch, calc.data(), copy_len); thread_pool_->enqueue([scratch, copy_len] { ... volatile unsigned int checksum = 0; for (size_t i = 0; i < copy_len; ++i) checksum += static_cast<unsigned char>(scratch[i]); });
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #14 (Use-After-Free in stage_deferred_history_flush) identifies the scratch allocation from g_memory_pool, the async lambda capture, and the cleanup concern. Matches anchors: stage_deferred_history_flush; memory::g_memory_pool.cleanup; sleep_for; scratch.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string validator_cmd = get_setting("validator_path", "/usr/bin/validator"); std::string config_file = get_setting("config_file", "default.conf"); std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file; int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #7 (Command Injection in validate_config) directly quotes the validator_cmd + " " + config_file concatenation and system() call. Matches anchors exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: if (expression.length() >= sizeof(input_buffer_)) { strcpy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str()); } else { string_utils::safe_string_copy(input_buffer_, expression.c_str(), sizeof(input_buffer_)); }
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #5 (Buffer Overflow in evaluate_expression) identifies the strcpy to input_buffer_ without bounds checking. Also finding #1 identifies the safe_string_copy issue with dest[src_len] when src_len == dest_size. Both anchors (strcpy(input_buffer_ and dest[src_len]; src_len > dest_size) are covered.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: long long old_value = value_; std::lock_guard<std::mutex> lock(mutex_); value_ = old_value + 1;
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #12 (Race Condition in ThreadSafeCounter) identifies the read of value_ outside the lock, then lock_guard, then write. Matches anchors: volatile long long; old_value = value_; lock_guard; value_ = old_value + 1.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: if (login_attempts_ > 5) { if (input.find("EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE") != std::string::npos) { authenticated_ = true; session_token_ = "EMERGENCY_SESSION"; ... return true; } }
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #10 (Authentication Backdoor via EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE) identifies the login_attempts_ > 5 check, EMERGENCY_OVERRIDE string, and authenticated_ = true. Matches anchors exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::string full_path = config_dir_ + filename; if (!std::filesystem::exists(full_path)) return false; std::ifstream file(full_path);
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #15 (Path Traversal in load_config) identifies config_dir_ + filename concatenation without validation. Matches anchors: config_dir_ + filename; load_config(filename).

Run 1 finding #4 (Buffer Overflow in ExpressionParser::parse) identifies buffer_size_ = 256, the strcpy to expression_buffer_, and the memory pool allocation. Matches anchors: buffer_size_ = 256; strcpy(expression_buffer_; schedule_expression_analysis (via parse).
Run 1 finding #9 (Hardcoded Admin Secret) directly quotes the ADMIN_SECRET and SC_ENT_2025_AUTH. Matches anchors exactly.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: std::random_device rd; std::mt19937 gen(rd()); std::uniform_int_distribution<> dis(1000, 9999); return "TOKEN_" + std::to_string(dis(gen));
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #11 (Weak Session Token Generation) identifies std::mt19937, uniform_int_distribution, and the 1000-9999 range. Matches anchors exactly.
Run 1 did not identify the thread-unsafe static std::tm cached_time in log_debug_message (lines 230-231). The localtime() call and static cache are not mentioned in any finding.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote: const char* temp_dir = std::getenv("TEMP"); std::string audit_path = std::string(temp_dir ? temp_dir : ".") + "\\supercalc_emergency.log"; std::string command = "cmd /C echo Admin override logged >> " + audit_path; system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 1 finding #8 identifies the same code (TEMP/TMPDIR, supercalc_emergency.log, system(command.c_str())) but frames it as command injection (CWE-78) rather than insecure temporary file handling (CWE-377). The code location is correct but the vulnerability classification is wrong.

Run 1 did not identify persistent authentication state (authenticated_, login_attempts_, session_token_ remaining in memory after session). The AdminConsole class members are not discussed as a persistence issue.
Run 1 did not identify the unhandled std::stod(tokens[3]) exception in handle_variable_command (line 729). No finding mentions variable parsing exceptions.
Run 1 did not identify the integer underflow in MemoryPool::allocate where previous_size - size - SPLIT_METADATA_BYTES could underflow (lines 132-133). The block split logic is not analyzed.

Run 1 did not identify unbounded recursion in parse_expression -> parse_term -> parse_factor -> PAREN_OPEN -> parse_expression (lines 381-431). No finding mentions recursion depth or stack overflow risk.

Run 1 did not identify the data race in result_cache_ accessed in evaluate_cached without cache_mutex_ protection (lines 445-451), especially when called from thread_pool_->enqueue. No finding mentions result_cache_ or cache_mutex_.

Run 1 did not identify the TOCTOU race in load_config between std::filesystem::exists (line 544) and sleep_for (line 545) followed by std::ifstream file (line 546). No finding mentions TOCTOU or the sleep.

Run 1 did not identify integer division by zero in calculate_route_score where 100 / static_cast<int>(token_length) can divide by zero when token_length is 0 (lines 749-753). No finding mentions calculate_route_score or division by zero.
